experiment_design_&_a/b_testing.question: What is an A/B test?
Why are A/B tests important in data-driven decision making?
What is the difference between a control group and a treatment group?
What is a hypothesis in an experiment?
Why is randomization important in A/B testing?
What is statistical significance?
What is a p-value?
Why is sample size important in experiments?
What is a success metric in an A/B test?
Why shouldn't business decisions rely only on intuition?
How would you design an A/B test for a new product feature?
How would you select the primary success metric for an experiment?
Explain Type I and Type II errors in hypothesis testing.
How would you determine an appropriate sample size before starting an experiment?
How would you reduce bias when designing experiments?
How would you determine whether an experiment should continue or stop early?
How would you evaluate conflicting experiment results across different customer segments?
How would you explain experiment results to non-technical stakeholders?
How would you validate that randomization worked correctly?
How would you identify confounding variables that might affect an experiment?
Design a complete A/B testing framework for launching a major product feature.
How would you determine whether statistically significant results are also meaningful from a business perspective?
How would you design experiments when random assignment is not possible?
Explain the trade-offs between experiment duration, sample size, statistical power, and business urgency.
How would you detect and correct for sample ratio mismatch in an A/B test?
How would you evaluate multiple experiments running simultaneously without introducing interference?
How would you prevent p-hacking and other practices that could invalidate experimental results?
How would you design experiments for products with low traffic where collecting large sample sizes is difficult?
Explain how sequential testing differs from traditional A/B testing.
Design an experimentation platform capable of supporting hundreds of simultaneous business experiments.
A new homepage design increases click-through rate but decreases purchases. Walk me through how you would determine whether it should be launched.
An A/B test produces statistically insignificant results after two weeks. Explain how you would decide whether to continue or stop the experiment.
Marketing wants to end an experiment early because initial results look promising. Describe how you would evaluate this request.
An experiment improves results for new users but harms experienced users. Explain how you would recommend moving forward.
A product manager asks you to test five new features simultaneously. Walk me through how you would design the experiments.
An experiment shows a statistically significant improvement of only 0.2%. Explain how you would determine whether deployment is justified.
A stakeholder questions whether the experiment results can be trusted because customer behavior changed during a holiday period. Describe your investigation.
An online experiment unexpectedly reduces company revenue despite improving its primary success metric. Explain how you would investigate.
Different teams interpret the same experiment results differently. Walk me through how you would reach a final recommendation.

You're responsible for the experimentation platform used across an entire organization. Explain how you would design experiment governance, statistical validation, monitoring, reporting, and decision-making processes.
